rationale: Integration lens, commit 1ad0d796, read-only. Since this branch predates 110's merge, I checked ms-panel.js's buildCommand against 110's ACTUAL merged CLI (origin/main:cmd/mindspec/panel.go, PR #184) rather than only the plan text. Exact match: `panel create <slug> --spec <id> --target <ref> [--bead <id>] [--round N]` == buildCommand's CMD_PANEL_CREATE template (flag order, optional --bead, positional slug); `panel verify <slug>` / `panel tally <slug>` == the CMD_PANEL_VERIFY/CMD_PANEL_TALLY templates (bare positional, no flags). The reported-directory contract the registration agent step is told to read from stdout (`fmt.Fprintf(...'panel directory: %s\n', dir)` in panel.go) is exactly the line panel create emits -- not a guessed format. R3b ladder (parse-failure once-then-MISSING, multi-object/narrative-wrapped rejected, quota-wall-only substitution eligibility) and R4 (claude-sub reviewer_id, fail-closed default via `args.claude_sub_on_quota === true`) match 110/109's documented artifact semantics. Step 5 returns `panel verify`+`panel tally` stdout verbatim via the {stdout} schema, doing zero consolidation and never stringing `mindspec complete` anywhere in the file -- confirmed by reading the whole 526-line script, not just grepping. Bead 3 (plan.md:811-891) gets a clean surface: the workflow's returned `{panelDir, slotResults, verifyOutput, tallyOutput}` maps directly onto what ms-panel-tally's planned note describes ('the per-slot verdict table + decision arrive pre-rendered in the workflow result, an already-run mindspec panel tally') and ms-panel-run's planned invocation shape `{slug, spec, target, bead_id?, round, lenses[], mix}` is a strict subset of args this script accepts (optional sha/claude_sub_on_quota both have documented, correct fail-safe defaults when omitted, per the script's own header comment and Step 2's `args.claude_sub_on_quota === true` check).
findings: Verified against origin/main:cmd/mindspec/panel.go (110 merged, PR #184) not just the plan text: create/verify/tally flag grammar, positional slug, and the 'panel directory: %s' stdout line all match buildCommand + the registration agent-step prompt exactly

panel tally exits non-zero on a Block decision (per panel.go's tallyExitAction contract); the Step-5 agent prompt asks only to 'return its complete stdout verbatim' without an explicit instruction to capture stdout regardless of exit code -- low-severity, not demonstrable as broken from static reading alone (Claude Code's shell tool conventionally exposes stdout independent of exit code, and the plan's own Manual e2e run 3 exercises exactly this Block path), but worth an explicit e2e confirmation once this branch is rebased past 110 rather than assumed

The registration prompt asks the agent to 'find the directory path it reports' in prose rather than citing the literal 'panel directory:' prefix -- the schema-constrained {panelDir} return and the single unambiguous line in create's stdout make this low-risk, but citing the literal prefix would remove any reliance on the agent's own parsing judgment
No invocation of buildCommand outside the four ALLOWED_CLI-derived templates found on a full read of the 526-line file; no second command-construction path for a mix slot or the codex wrapper

Bead 3's planned ms-panel-run invocation shape and ms-panel-tally's planned pre-rendered-result note both consume exactly the fields this workflow's contract documents/returns -- no dangling reference
